sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Manchester City have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 3 wins and 2 losses, scoring an average of 2.2 goals per game. Napoli, on the other hand, are in excellent form, winning all of their last 5 matches and averaging 2.4 goals per game.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters in 2017, Manchester City won both matches against Napoli with scores of 2-1 and 4-2, indicating a historical edge in high-scoring games. Both teams have consistently scored in these matchups.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Erling Haaland has been in exceptional form for Manchester City with 6 goals in the last 5 matches, while Napoli's Rasmus Højlund and Kevin De Bruyne are also contributing significantly with high ratings and goals.

sections.subtitle: Match Statistics
sections.text: Both teams have tendencies for games with over 2.5 goals, with Manchester City often scoring first and Napoli showing strong first-half performances. Corners and cards are likely to be within moderate ranges based on recent stats.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the score remains level at 0-0 between Manchester City and Napoli at the Etihad Stadium. Despite the deadlock, Manchester City have dominated possession and created numerous opportunities. Napoli, on the other hand, have struggled to make an impact, further hampered by a red card that leaves them with ten players for the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Manchester City's dominance
halftimeSections.text: Manchester City have controlled the game with significant possession and territorial advantage. They've had multiple entries into the final third and penalty area, showcasing their intent to break down Napoli's defense. Their expected goals figure reflects a team that has been knocking on the door, with players like Erling Haaland and Phil Foden heavily involved in the attack. Defensively, they've been solid, winning a good share of duels and aerial battles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Napoli's struggles
halftimeSections.text: Napoli have found it tough to get a foothold in this match. With limited possession and fewer entries into the final third, their attacking threat has been minimal. The red card to Giovanni Di Lorenzo is a major blow, reducing their numbers and likely forcing a more defensive approach in the second half. Despite creating one big chance, they failed to convert, and their expected goals are significantly lower than City's.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Manchester City have shown consistent attacking momentum throughout the first half, with frequent spells of pressure in Napoli's half. Their attacking intent rarely waned, as they pushed forward with purpose in multiple phases of play. Napoli, in contrast, had only brief moments of attacking momentum, with a short period where they managed to push back, but it quickly fizzled out as City's dominance reasserted itself.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Manchester City, Erling Haaland remains the focal point of their attack, constantly threatening despite not finding the net yet. Phil Foden and Bernardo Silva have also been creative forces, with notable contributions in key passes. On Napoli's side, Matteo Politano showed glimpses of danger with a big chance created, while their goalkeeper Vanja Milinković-Savić has been crucial in keeping the scoreline level.
